# Diffusion Policy and Variable Admittance {.section-divider}

## Why Combine Them?

- Diffusion Policy can model multimodal task strategies.
- Variable admittance can shape how the robot physically interacts.
- The combined problem asks: what should the robot do, and how compliant should it be?

::: {.notes}
Main point: motivate the combined architecture as coupling decision-making with interaction behavior.

Intuition: for the same insertion task, the robot may need both a trajectory choice and a compliance profile.

Warning: do not imply the diffusion model should replace the controller; it can provide references or parameters that a controller enforces.
:::

---

## Architecture: Desired Trajectories

The diffusion policy outputs desired motion:

::: {.equation-card}
$$
\mathbf{x}^{des}_{t:t+H}
\sim
p_\theta(\mathbf{x}^{des}_{t:t+H} \mid \mathbf{o}_{t-T_o:t})
$$
:::

An admittance controller handles contact response.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
<div class="placeholder-title">Policy outputs desired trajectory</div>
<div class="placeholder-path">assets/figures/dp_outputs_trajectory.png</div>
:::

::: {.notes}
Main point: this is the conservative architecture: learn desired motion and keep compliance in a separate controller.

Intuition: the diffusion policy says where to go; the admittance controller decides how measured force modifies that motion.

Warning: this is easier to make safe, but may limit how much task-specific compliance the policy can express.
:::

---

## Architecture: Trajectory and Compliance Parameters

The diffusion policy can also output motion and control parameters:

::: {.equation-card}
$$
(\mathbf{x}^{des}_{t:t+H}, \mathbf{K}_{t:t+H}, \mathbf{D}_{t:t+H})
\sim
p_\theta(\cdot \mid \mathbf{o}_{t-T_o:t})
$$
:::

The controller then enforces bounds before execution.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
<div class="placeholder-title">Policy outputs trajectory and gains</div>
<div class="placeholder-path">assets/figures/dp_outputs_admittance_parameters.png</div>
:::

::: {.notes}
Main point: this architecture lets the policy generate both motion and interaction parameters.

Intuition: the policy can learn that some task phases require high stiffness while others require yielding behavior.

Warning: outputting gains directly is high risk unless bounds, smoothing, and stability checks are enforced.
:::

---

## Sensor Modalities

Potential conditioning signals:

::: {.two-column-balanced}
::: {.compact}
- vision for scene context
- force/torque for interaction state
- proprioception for robot state
- EMG for human intent or assistance cues
:::

::: {.figure-placeholder}
<div class="placeholder-title">Multimodal robot sensing</div>
<div class="placeholder-path">assets/figures/multimodal_robot_sensing.png</div>
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Main point: multimodal observations are useful because contact and human intent are not fully visible from images alone.

Intuition: vision tells where objects are, force tells what interaction is happening, and EMG may hint at intended human assistance.

Warning: more modalities increase synchronization and calibration complexity; noisy signals can mislead the policy.
:::

---

## Safety Bounds for Learned Parameters

Learned gains should be projected or constrained:

::: {.equation-card}
$$
K_{min} \preceq K_d \preceq K_{max},
\qquad
D_{min} \preceq D_d \preceq D_{max}
$$
:::

This keeps the learned policy inside an admissible control envelope.

::: {.notes}
Main point: learned compliance parameters should be projected into a safe admissible set.

Intuition: think of the neural network as proposing values and the controller as clipping or projecting them before execution.

Warning: simple clipping may not be enough; rate limits and coupled constraints between stiffness and damping may also matter.
:::

---

## Connection to My Research Direction

Possible research questions:

- Can diffusion policies learn when to be stiff or compliant?
- How should force and EMG signals condition the policy?
- What safety filters are needed for learned admittance parameters?
- How do we evaluate performance beyond task success?

::: {.figure-placeholder}
<div class="placeholder-title">Research direction system diagram</div>
<div class="placeholder-path">assets/figures/research_direction_system.png</div>
:::

::: {.notes}
Main point: close the technical arc by positioning the lecture as a research direction.

Intuition: the open problem is not only better imitation, but learned interaction policies that remain safe and interpretable.

Warning: be explicit about what is speculative versus already demonstrated in the literature or in your own work.
:::
